reviews.text:
I find Mr Atkerson to be highly unethical. I retained Mr. Atkerson to pursue a matter on contingency with an offer in hand that was unacceptable. I confirmed - prior to our engagement -  that per his contract he would not apply the contingency if they did not change their offer. Mr. Atkerson refused to take the matter to trial under our contract terms and on his last contact with the defendant they asserted they would not change their offer - so I terminated his representation as per our contract. I retained more effective representation who achieved an acceptable settlement. Mr Atkerson then filed a lien against my settlement and refuses to release the held funds - so he is now a defendant in a suit I have filed to have the remaining settlement funds released to me.
EDIT: Considering Mr Atkerson's response - my attorney tried to get him to release the funds subject to lien from the escrow account for 8 months and filed suit in Collin County in May 2024. Our initial process server failed to execute service and a second server was retained last month to ensure Mr. Atkerson was served. I am confident in the facts as I have represented them given the written communications from Mr. Atkerson proving each point. Mr Atkerson is quite frankly being disingenuous in saying I have been paid as he has he has not release the lien he fraudulently applied and I have  not been paid the remaining settlement amounts held by his fraudulent lien.
reviews.ownerResponse: I have not been paid a dime by this person although he received a settlement after I spent considerable time and effort on his case over several months of time. This is a false, libelous and defamatory review full of lies for which I will now be forced to file a libel lawsuit for damages as I expended substantial time and effort on this person's behalf on clearly stated terms for a contingent attorney fee on any and all recovery with my fee calculation capped by settlement offer I obtained during my representation of and with my contingent fee to be paid upon any subsequent recovery.   I also even told him as documented via email that my contingent fee as stated would apply to any offer I generated during my representation and as stated plainly therein in the representation agreement he signed and which he further acknowledged.  My contingent fee clearly applied to any and all recovery and was not limited just to any specific recovery over and above a certain amount, whether that be a stated amount or some other amount never expressly stated or ever agreed to.  It is disingenuous for this person now to conveniently claim otherwise after the fact to defraud me and cheat me out of my contracted fee. My friend referred me to a law firm that specializes in labor disputes, representing both employers and employees. I stated my case and spent two weeks going back and forth with the paralegal, who was in contact with the attorney, before they finally quoted me their prices. Unfortunately, the quote suggested they assumed I was a large corporation with deep pockets, which I am not.

The day before receiving their quote, not wanting to deal with a firm that seemed uninterested in taking my case seriously, I searched for labor lawyers in the Dallas area using ChatGPT. Dan Atkerson's practice was at the top of the list. After visiting his website and watching a couple of his videos, my gut told me he was the right choice. My gut was right.

Dan listened to me, truly heard me, and gave me excellent advice. I knew I could trust his sage opinion. Once he took my case, he provided outstanding representation. I hired Dylan Davis to represent me in JP court. Unfortunately, he arrived late to the hearing and did not appear fully prepared. The case materials were not organized, and I ended up presenting all of the supporting documentation myself.

After the unfavorable ruling, I raised concerns about the level of preparation and asked about next steps. I was initially told he would not be moving forward with the matter. When I requested a refund of the $500 fee due to concerns about the representation, the response I received in his text included statements such as “Go for it” and “Not going to be extorted for fees.”

While I understand that outcomes cannot be guaranteed, professionalism, preparation, and communication matter. Based on my experience, I would have expected a higher standard of representation.
reviews.ownerResponse: Greg's review could not be more dishonest. If you are late or absent for a civil hearing a Default Judgment is Ordered and our case was heard as scheduled. Greg brought his "case materials" as parties do in JP Court, so "organization" was not even tasked to the firm. Greg threatened to defame us online if we did not refund his fee for the outcome of his case. As he points out in his review, he was hopeful we would work with him further but we declined due to anticipation of this type of behavior. We are pleased that we ended the relationship on our own terms.

I contacted this attorney seeking representation in an active civil matter. During our phone call, I attempted to explain an important aspect of my case, but my explanations were repeatedly dismissed, and I felt shut down rather than heard despite my efforts to clarify.

The conversation became unproductive, and after the call ended, I received a text message characterizing my response as rude. The focus remained on explaining that documents not signed by a judge are generally not considered court orders. While that point may be true in a court context, it did not address the specific issue I was raising, which involved a state agency’s Agreed Order.

After the call, I explained the same issue in writing via text message. Once it was clarified in writing, he acknowledged understanding the document and indicated he would represent me at trial. I then promptly emailed all requested case documents the same day for review.

Despite that, I never received any response, acknowledgment, or follow-up after sending the materials. Communication, attentiveness, and follow-through are critical when evaluating legal representation, and based on my experience, those expectations were not met.

Response to the firms reply: For clarity, there was no argument. I was attempting to explain a specific document relevant to my case, and the discussion became unproductive because we were not aligned on that issue and continuing it did not feel constructive. At that point, I chose to end the call. I did not seek further interaction after that; the firm initiated follow-up by text, at which point I clarified the matter in writing. My review reflects my experience with the communication process and the lack of follow-up after documents were sent.

That’s it.

I cannot recommend this attorney based on our experience. After initially agreeing to assist with our legal matter, communication became problematic and the representation never materialized. Most concerning, we were later informed that he intended to work with the opposing party in our case.

We have documented several issues:

- Initial consultation and agreement to represent us, followed by extended delays
- Lack of clear communication about case status
- Stated intention to align with the opposing party
- A federal complaint has been filed against this attorney, which he appealed unsuccessfully
- Subsequent threats of legal action against us for posting this review

There appear to be ongoing regulatory issues with the Texas State Bar that potential clients should be aware of. The attorney has made conflicting statements about whether we were clients—claiming no attorney-client relationship existed while simultaneously asserting our communications were “confidential.”

The delays in receiving services contributed to our case escalating to federal court when it might have been resolved at an earlier stage. Our counter-claims have since been approved by the court.

I encourage anyone considering this attorney to thoroughly research their disciplinary history and proceed carefully.

Previously, I left only left low stars but no review, but since then, they have decided to engage with me via this review and by telephone demanding I remove this, I will now fully tell the story.

I am a member of a MetLife Legal plan and recently I was served with papers regarding an old debt by a "creditor" or collections that I have never done business with. Therefore, I utilized MetLife Legal Plan to find an attorney. The first option they provided was Manchee & Manchee. It clearly states that Debt Collection Defense is one of their services. (See first image below) In the response below he states that he is not responsible for third party sites (I might add that he chose to participate in and be a member of that network), okay fine! But even on his own website, he also suggests the same service. This is a bit misleading. (See second image in blue).

I went to the "office" here in Arlington, Texas to establish a client relationship. There were two receptionist at the front desk. I told them my situation and they advised me "this is a virtual office and he does not really come in to this location." She told me I could leave the service papers there and they would give them to him to look at and he would get back with me. They were copies of the original, so I did not mind. I also called the "office" and left a voice mail about what transpired at the Arlington "office". He sends a me text message and calls frantically saying he does not offer debt collection defense. I was said fine and went back to the so called "office" and picked up the copies and that was it. I was a bit perturbed by the misleading website offerings so I left two stars and nothing more was said on here.

Last week I receive a call from MetLife stating Mr Manchee has complained about my two stars and demanded I removed them. That is the trigger for how all this has devolved into this situation.

I have been a marketing and public relations professional for 25 years and this firm seems to struggle with how to handle consumer relations and other public discourse. I would be happy to remove this if he was a bit more honest about his offerings or at least apologized.

In the end, I have a limited window here and went ahead and filed my own Answer to the JP court pro se. I have also found legal council since then as well.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Justin, it's unfortunate that you feel we didn't provide you with the service you required.  Your voicemail indicated that you dropped off documents at one of our offices without calling us first.  Unfortunately, your legal concerns were not within the parameters of our firm's areas of practice. Had you called us first, this problem would have been avoided.  Our records indicate that when you did call and leave a message, we promptly returned your call and advised you that your issues were outside of our areas of practice.  It is also our understanding that you found our firm’s information on a third-party website that did not list that office visits are by appointment only.   We have no control over third-party websites, but our Google listing indicates that we meet clients at any of our offices by appointment only.   We hope you have been able to find representation regarding your legal matter.

We hired this firm for our bankruptcy, and to be fair, the case was successful and ultimately discharged. However, the communication throughout the process has been incredibly frustrating.

Trying to get anyone from the office to respond to emails or answer the phone is like pulling teeth. We’ve had to send multiple emails just to get a response, and calls and voicemails often go unanswered. Going through bankruptcy is already stressful, and the lack of communication has made the process even more difficult than it needed to be.

Even now that the case has been discharged, we are still waiting for our final discharge paperwork and have had difficulty getting responses from the office about it. Basic communication and follow-through would make a big difference for clients going through an already stressful situation.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I am sorry you are frustrated. The chapter 7 process takes about 3 and a half months and most of the work and communication occurs before the case is even filed. Once it is filed, there is a lot of waiting and not much is happening.

I understand that you would like regular updates, but during the bankruptcy process there are months where nothing is happening and so there is nothing to update you on. It isn't that we aren't communicating important information. There is just nothing to report while we wait for the judge to sign the discharge order.

You should receive your discharge order sixty to seventy-five days after your meeting with the trustee. Once it is signed, we will send you an email letting you know that it has been signed by the judge and information on improving your credit score after receiving a discharge. If you do have questions, please feel free to call me directly at 214-618-1705.

Nathan Graham
Graham Legal, PLLC

reviews.text:
I recently filed for bankruptcy (Just discharged!). I checked out a few different law firms, and when speaking with one of the lawyers from Graham legal, they just seemed like they gave me the best feeling if that makes sense. The lawyer i spoke with was very knowledgeable and reassuring. After I began, they were able to answer any and all of my questions, help me with processing the paperwork, and walked me through the entire process.

We conducted several inquiries and this establishment seemed suitable for our Chapter 7 Bankruptcy. We initiated a preliminary meeting to ascertain our eligibility. We submitted all our documents. The hearing was conducted and it was a positive experience, lasting only 5 minutes. It is essential to remain patient and avoid any anxiety.

Bankruptcy is scary. Never thought I would have to file. However, with my husband losing his leg, bills just became too much on one income. Thankfully, Graham Legal was able to help us go through the process smoothly and efficiently. Contact was superb and they answered every question we had.

Called and left a detailed message with my name and phone number. Got a call 3 minutes later. The gentleman on the phone never listened to the message I left so he didn’t know my name or why I called. What is the point of leaving a message if you don’t listen to it. Very unprofessional. A good law firm is about the details. Very disappointed as this firm gets good reviews. Need to do much better with the opening process of obtaining a client. Calling a potential client back by using his name is a good start.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Joseph, Let me make sure I understand. You called our office, someone realized you called and didn't answer quick enough and missed your call, so they immediately called you back. You are upset that they didn't listen to your voicemail before calling you back.

We, like many law firms use voice IP phones. Messages left as voicemail are emailed to us by the phone provider. Sometimes those emails take a few minutes to arrive, so if we called you back in 3 minutes, we probably called you before receiving your voicemail.

I am sorry you are upset that we immediately called you back. Most of our clients are happy to receive a prompt return phone call. But I now understand that some people do not like their phone calls to be returned promptly.

Nathan Graham
Graham Legal, PLLC

Lorena, I am sorry you are upset.  What you are requesting is not possible.  I don't make the laws.  I just follow them.  I am happy to talk to you about your concerns any time you like, but I am bound by the court's rules and procedures.  I wish you hadn't given us a 1 star review for something I have no control over, but I also understand you are frustrated.  I hope things improve.

They handled our case and did a good job we haven't had any issues so far I'll update this if anything happens but they did a good job. I don't know what other questions people would have about filing bankruptcy some of them charge extra for everything this place has a flat rate I'm not going to quote their rate here because it may change in the future. What I will say is their flat rate was cheaper than most places base rate plus expenses I called around before I picked them as my attorney. But an example of something to be careful of is other places will charge as much as $500 to fax or make additional efforts to make creditors leave you alone so if they have to fax them or call them or send them a letter or anything like that they're going to charge you a lot of money to do those things they also charge additional preparation fees and things like this separate from their base fee most of them started a base fee of 2 to 3,000 and it goes up from there. When I say Graham legal was good it means they did what they're supposed to do without trying to charge me extra. Are there cheaper lawyers there might be but you get what you pay for believe me I know people that have hired these so-called cheaper lawyers and it wasn't cheaper later.

Graham Legal was outstanding in guiding us through the process of bankruptcy. Every single person we dealt with was professional and courteous and very knowledgeable. Please, if you put your faith in this law firm to help and guide you, you will never regret it. We HIGHLY recommend this firm.

I absolutely recommend Graham Legal to handle your Bankruptcy, they do no judgment at all , I was embarrassed by my actions and what it led  to be but they never made me feel less than , not only that they offer payment options and they also give you guidance on the after math of you bankruptcy I give this place 10 stars if I could
I had a great experience with Graham Legal. The team guided me through every step, explained everything clearly, and made a stressful process manageable.
Graham legal made the process very easy. They always respond to emails and phones in a professional manner.
Highly recommend Graham Legal.  The staff is professional, friendly, and efficient.

i phoned several firms and was discouraged and lost all hope.  graham legal took my case and  worked with me very respectful and turned a difficult situation into  a success  for me. i can only recommend them.

I don’t know where to start! From day one Megan & Nathan have been nothing but friendly. The assistants also have been good. I’m recommending them to everyone I know. This is a great firm! You won’t be disappointed at all.!
Graham Legal did a fantastic job with my bankruptcy case. Mr Graham and his staff were very organized and thorough.

I highly recommend Graham Legal for anyone considering filing for bankruptcy. The process was smooth and stress-free, with affordable options and flexible payment arrangements. Nathan was there every step of the way, providing updates and guiding me through the entire process.
I would like thank Mr. Graham and all his staff members for everything thank you.
Very easy to work with, communication is excellent. They never rushed when I had questions and always kept me in the loop

Highly recommended!

Filing for bankruptcy was easy, quick, great communication and wonderful assistance. Anytime I had questions, I was responded to quickly. Every step in the process was explained clearly and also was given advice how to help improve credit score once everything was finalized with court. Again, highly recommend!

I feel very ignored. I’ve emailed several times about my case and do not get a response. My payment was fast approaching to the debt collector but in the system, they still had the old numbers. I had no way to pay them on what was decided. When I contacted them, they said they couldn’t speak to me because I had lawyer representation. When I emailed Graham Legal back, they said they should be able to. Tried again and received the same message back from the debt collector. Luckily, with no help from Graham Legal, I was able to just keep logging into the debt collector site and eventually they had the correct payment plan. Just disappointed.

**In response to Nathan's reply: I am fully aware you are not the same company. BUT who am I supposed to reach out to in regards to making an on time payment if they say they cannot speak with me since I have lawyer representation? It was getting close to my payment date and I reached out to them twice because that's what your company told me to do. Kathlyn I believe is her name told me they should be able to speak with me regarding the account and asked me to try again. I did and then Kathlyn fell off the face of the earth. I emailed her back June 24th (the same day she told me to try again) and then again on July 2nd. I was freaking out. I am glad it worked out as well but I just feel like your company could have done better. We are paying you to represent me.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Lorelei,

I am sorry you are disappointed. I am not sure how we could have helped you with that problem. It sounds like the debt collector was a little slow to update their system. We are two different companies. We have no control over their employees or how quickly they update their records. I am glad to hear that the issue has now been resolved. If you need anything else, please feel free to reach out. If it is something that we have control over, then we are happy to help you with it.

Thanks,

Nathan Graham

reviews.text:
We recently worked with Nathan on our Chapter 7 bankruptcy, and we couldn’t be more grateful for the professionalism, clarity, and support throughout the entire process. From the very beginning, he was kind, nonjudgmental, and took the time to explain everything in a way we could easily understand.

Even after the discharge, he went above and beyond by providing us with helpful resources to rebuild our credit on our own—no upsells or pressure to pay for extra services. That level of honesty and care is rare.

If you're feeling overwhelmed or unsure about bankruptcy, this is the attorney you want on your side. Highly recommend

I would like to thank the staff at Graham Legal.  I research other bankruptcy and then call them.  Graham Legal seemed most friendly and was able to assist me all the way.  If I had any questions, they answer either through email or a phone call.  I truly was very patient with them when they did not answer the phone. It was worth the wait.  If you’re on the sideline about choosing a bankruptcy lawyer, please choose Graham Legal.  They even have payment plans and certainly cheaper than most Attorney I called.  Just have all your documents in place so it can go smoothly for you.  Once again, thank you for help turning my life around. -CTW

Graham Legal was one of very few attorneys I found that offered a very reasonable payment plan for retaining their services. But the Pièce de résistance was how effortless the whole experience was. Very easy affordable. my case was complicated but they dealt with everything quickly and efficiently.  All online great communication.

Graham Legal was outstanding and guided us through the process and made it very easy to deal with, during a difficult time. Thank you

reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Amber.  It appears that we did something that made you unhappy with our law firm. I can be reached at 214-618-1705.  I look forward to hearing from you.

reviews.text: Mr. Angelino helped me and our family navigate the judicial system for my incarcerated loved one with ease. Mr. Angelino always treated us with respect, clarity and professionalism. Thank you so much for all you did to help with my loved one’s case, from transport to a quick speedy plea deal, he was there every step of the way!

My court was in 2021 and now I had to go after another lawyer to remove my DWI in the background check and I discover that now I can't do that, I will have the DWI in my history for the rest of my life, because Attorney James Angelino never offered me this service after my court. I didn't know I would have to do this and I wasn't instructed to do it and now my background check will be dirty for the rest of my life. I had a very good lawyer!
The best lawyer ever. Very responsive. Very respectful. Most importantly does an exceptional job.

If I could give more stars I would, he is an excellent Lawyer, he handled my son's case, he had more than 12 charges and he managed to knock down more than half of them, he was facing more than 20 years in prison and he only managed to get my son to serve 30 days, I will be grateful to him for life, he also handled the case of a child who to me is like my son, since his family turned their backs on him, he caused an accident where a person lost their life, I decided to help him, I consulted many lawyers, they all told me that they would give him 30 years in prison or that maybe he would do life in prison, something that broke my heart, he was able to help me, he investigated the case a lot, he helped him a lot and managed to get him only 15 years, he is just a child, he was 18 years old, and he did not allow so much injustice, I am very grateful that your profession is honest, I will be grateful to you for life. I recommend it 100%. Don't hesitate to consult it. It will help you in the most difficult cases. Thanks, Jimmy.
Extremely knowledgeable and reliable  professional with integrity. Always quick to respond the second someone reaches out. Well known for being an overall beyond amazing lawyer.
James & his team are the best in the business and wonderful to work with. He has helped me time after time & I am forever thankful for him.
Mr. Angelino was very responsive and effective in representing me.
Thank you so much! I’ll never forget everything you’ve done for us.
Outstanding support and resolution to my issues. I found James and team highly responsive and professional. Highly recommended

Geesh why is it so hard these days for people to actually do a good job! I had made it very clear that I didn't mind paying the $300 hr fee if they were going to be beneficial on MY BEHALF!! It's like she worked for the other attorney and the landlord.  After disengaging I was able to get done what she failed to do.  It makes me wonder if hiring attorneys these days are even worth it.  I guess my mistake was hiring a firm that obviously are used to dealing with people and companies with unlimited funds.

Be prepared for Girling Law to ghost you when you have a dispute regarding thier service or costs.  I hired them for
an eviction in August of last year.  I had already posted the eviction notice to the tenant but since I was an out of state landlord, decided to hire Marc.  Other than the free initial consultation from Marc, the only other work the firm did for me was send an email to me asking for tenant detail and payment history. I supplied info but apparently since I did not send one or more of the documents via the correct format  they asked me to resend which I never did because within (1) week after signing with the firm, my tenant left the property.  I informed the firm that thier service was no longer necessary. I had paid the initial  $750 at this point and asked for any adjustment of fee because other than punching a few pieces of information into thier system, they did not do much of anything  else. Para legal, Victoria Martinez, said that she would ask Marc and get back to me that next day.  She never did. Then  another draft of funds was taken!  In total I paid $950 for very little work. I continued to get ahold of Victoria and line just rang with no vm options.  I was able to reach others in the billing department who would leave messages for Victoria but she was unresponsive this went on for over a month and a half. asked for my file and documents were incomplete. Eventually, I sent a dispute letter outlining all the call/email detail showing a lack of response.  I was informed that Marc denied my request because it had no merit.  Beware if you enter into contact with Girling Law.

After 6 months of working with them we hired another firm to take care of the eviction. They were able to complete the eviction within 45 days and mentioned the excuses Girling gave us were just their misunderstanding of the address process. They illustrated that Girling was incorrect by completing the eviction on the first try. Feel free to send me a message for the name of the attorney to use.

This man was hired to do my mother's will,by my sister & her husband is a relative,they then went & evicted me from my mother's home which I paid rent,they did this out of hate & greed,shame on you Marc get the facts this was against my mother's wishes she started having demitia in june you process an eviction if the Wiggins had asked me what my summer plans were I had deposit on a place in june it flooded so got another by sept 2.This was harrassement thru law firm I never received summons directly to me nor signed no certified letter.I was told by Mrs Wiggins not to tell my mother what she did  evil both , beware of what a family member will do once they get the power over someone's will in my case my mother she told me before she slipped into demitia she wanted to change her will!

Girling Law made a stressful situation so much easier to handle.  It was the eleventh hour heading into county court to get yet another judgement against a couple of  legal-wise tenants. The Girling team took control of the case and navigated me to victory despite last minute machinations of the tenants. If you need help with eviction matters, I  recommend Girling Law.

I wanted to pay in cash. initially he didn't agree, later he agreed saying if you pay in full cash is acceptable. Even though it is clearly there on the recording, he called me lier. I wish there is a way to attach the recording so that people can makeout who is the lier.

Do not use this firm for evictions. They will be responsive in the beginning and once they take your money, you won’t hear from them again you will only have administrators giving you information and telling you that someone from the paralegal team will call you back, but that will never happen. My Eviction happened on the 18th. It is now the 26th and still no follow up from an attorney about the positions of my case I had to go online For myself. I have called repeatedly every day every other day still no answer. In the beginning, they charge me twice and told me that I was getting my money back at the end of the case, I told them this was unacceptable and after constantly calling back and emailing I was able to get a refund for the double charge. They have multiple phone numbers so they can just keep rerouting your calls to voicemail and now returning your call once you have them.

Helped me evict a live in girlfriend who was not agreeing to leave in a timely manner. All it took was an official notice to vacate and was able to have her out in less than two weeks. Very easy to work with.

I Highly recommend Gurling Law. They were so helpful explaining the process. Thank you to Jacqueline for all her help. She answered all our questions in a timely manner.

Jackie helped me communicate with my tenant and made the process seamless. Marc and Jackie guided me through the eviction and assisted me every step of the way. If you need an eviction law firm I'd recommend this team as the way to go.

I found Girling Law Firm through a Google search. Marc Girling called back, himself. He is extremely knowledgeable of the law. He was very responsive in the initial phase (before we paid the retainer). He said there was a good chance that we would not have to appear for trial and we should have  possession of the house within 4 weeks. We did have to appear for trial and it has been over 4 weeks and we are still waiting to get in. I am a licensed real estate agent and property manager, that helps out my investors occasionally. It does not look good for me to have to continuously update him with contradicting information. It would have helped to know who my contact person was from the start and for better communication in the beginning. Once everything started to finally move along, Jackie became very involved with our case. I don't know when she became the person handling our case, but it was reassuring to have a name and number to tell the landlord. She was very professional and became very communicative as time went on. I think everyone in the office is very knowledgeable and pleasant to work with, but there is still a problem with overall communication (after reading other reviews, it makes sense to assume that they are still going through some reorganization within the company).  I will continue to use Girling Law Firm and more specifically, Jackie Nalu as my point of contact! I am overall happy with the service and would definitely recommend them! I have no doubt that any kinks will be worked out soon and I will be changing my 4 star to 5! (I give Jackie a 5 star rating all ready though, lol)

Marc responded quickly and provided a wealth of knowledge and advice for an eviction from a house in Plano that had become complex and threatening.  He was thorough, very knowledgeable and took the time to explain things that I did not know.  I will certainly call on him for help again if needed.
I give a thumbs up, and a 5 star review to Jackie Nablus and the team at the Girling Law Firm. I experienced nothing but prompt, caring and efficient service during my difficult eviction ordeal!

Jackie, from Girling Law Firm, was instrumental in navigating a highly complex eviction case involving a serial evictee who had skillfully evaded our thorough background check, concealing a history of evictions, lawsuits, poor credit, and a felony conviction. Despite the tenant’s expertise in exploiting legal loopholes—using appeals, pauper’s affidavits, and procedural challenges to delay the process—Jackie meticulously guided me through every step of the legal proceedings, keeping me informed with timely updates and clear explanations. Her diligence and proactive communication ensured the professional scammer was forcibly removed with minimal delay and damage to the property, culminating in a successful trial before the judge. I highly recommend Jackie and Girling Law Firm for their exceptional handling of complex eviction cases.

reviews.text: This guy runs a group on facebook titled “Ask a Texas Attorney” and his whole purpose in the group is to have people sign up for $279 consult. If you give advice on legal aid groups for low income people. He blocks you.

reviews.text:
First and foremost I would like to extend my sympathies to anyone dealing with the loss of a family member. It leaves an emptiness within you that I still can't quite explain. It became apparent very soon after my sons death that I was going to need to seek the help of an attorney to protect the rights of my grandson. I spoke to several personal acquaintances that told me there was nothing that could be done. After contacting different law groups that gave me similar advise after hearing of my concerns I was given Dominic's conntact number and called him.  A conference call was set up with Dominic and Terri. After hearing my concerns and the details as I new them, Dominic and Terri agreed to take on the case even though explaining to me that it would be an uphill battle. Dominic and Terri didn't just crest the hill they planted a flag on top of it. In my opinion 5 stars doesn't do them justice. They go beyond the legal matters, being very cariing and personable in regards to dealing with ones emotions in a case such as my own. I would highly recommend that anyone having to deal with any probate matters contact this firm and request a visit with Dominic and Terri. Thanks to them my grandsons rights were protected and my sons wishes will be carried out.

Thanks again,
Rex A.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Rex,
Thank you for sharing such a heartfelt review, and please accept our deepest condolences for the loss of your son. We know that pursuing legal action while grieving is incredibly difficult, and we are honored that you trusted Dominic and Terri to walk through this with you.

We’re grateful to hear that you felt supported both legally and emotionally, and that your grandson’s rights and your son’s wishes have been protected. Dominic and Terri care deeply about the families they serve, and your words mean a great deal to all of us here at the firm.

Thank you again for your trust and recommendation.

reviews.text:
Went to them about a prenup.  Ashley Teakell took my entry information and set up a phone consultation with Dana Palmer. During the consultation, I was given advice that was about a million miles from what I expected.  That advice was that a prenup was not right for my situation and that we should be in the marriage or not.  Surrender all or not.  Protect each other and not ourselves.

Dana even advised that I share the recording of our conversation with my fiance so that she could hear this same advise.  He is a very Christian man who believes wholeheartedly in marriage.

He could have very easily taken my "case" and gone thru that whole affair but instead chose to give strong counsel against it.  Every time I think of it I'm reminded of King David taking a census.  Joab advised strongly against it, David ignored him much to the detriment of Israel.  I think I'll take this good counsel.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing this thoughtful review. We’re glad Ashley was able to get you scheduled and that your conversation with Dana provided clarity and peace of mind. Our goal is always to offer honest counsel that aligns with your values and best interests, even when that means advising against moving forward with a legal document.

We’re truly honored by your trust and wish you and your fiancé a strong and healthy marriage.

reviews.text:
Highly unprofessional and it is unclear where my retainers have gone.

Initially they assured me they would file, not to worry. Over a month or two later I was surprised at my door, at night by a large male process server, an alarming situation for a single woman.  I finally heard back from the paralegal and complained they might have warned me as my ex filed at least a month ago AND there was no record of my lawyer having filed as my representation. The paralegal admitted she dropped the ball. When I challenged their billing, they subsequently admitted they significantly overcharged me. I sent them a detailed outline of my situation, but each time they seem to be unaware of key details of my case.

They did not seem to want to advocate for me and, when we were finally done, sent me a proof of a decree that included none of what was agreed. Additionally, there were several glaring errors that could have had significant long term consequences had i not caught them. Today was a major deadline and I have not heard from my lawyer despite 3 emails to their office. Additionally, prior to these issues I recommended this office to several other people who have all come back to me to let me know about unprofessional behavior, lack of communication,  and unclear billing.

Hire at your own risk.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Farrah,
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are sorry to hear you are still feeling frustrated despite our previous conversations and efforts to address your concerns. At Palmer Law Group, we remain committed to resolving issues in a professional and respectful manner. Please don’t hesitate to reach out again if you’d like to revisit this matter, we are happy to discuss it further in hopes of reaching an amicable resolution.

reviews.text:
Highly recommended! I would not waste my time or hire anyone other than this law firm!

I reached out to Palmer Law Group, P.C. late at night around 9 PM because I saw they were open on Google. To my surprise, I was able to speak directly with the intake specialist, Joyce Hernandez. I’ve spoken with many law firms over the past year, but I have never encountered a team as professional, patient, warm-hearted, understanding, diligent, kind, and—most importantly—humane as this one. Joyce was absolutely phenomenal.

She immediately scheduled me a same-night consultation with Mr. Palmer himself. I cannot even begin to describe how unique of a person and attorney he is. He is one of the most genuine attorneys you could ever meet. He structured the call in a clear, three-step order, showing just how organized and effective he is. He patiently listened to every detail of my situation, understood the complexities, and followed everything I explained with ease—which is a true sign of his competence.

It didn’t feel like I was just talking to an attorney. It felt like I was speaking with a family member or best friend who truly cares. That experience gave me hope after such a long period of stress and difficulty.

This law firm is honest, genuine, caring, and straightforward. They listen to what you want, they tell you honestly what they can do, and they are upfront about everything. I have never imagined having such a positive and uplifting experience with a law firm.

If you want a law firm that actually cares about you as a human being while also being incredibly competent and effective, Palmer Law Group, P.C. is the one
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for the wonderful review! We appreciate you and look forward to helping you get healthy results.

reviews.text: When I called Palmer Law Group seeking a consultation I discussed my circumstances with Ms. Bradley.   She immediately verified no conflicts of interests over the same phone call, explained to me that the confidential consultation would be recorded, and then offered me 3 options to meet with Mr. Palmer: phone, video conference​, or in-person.  I chose in-person and she was able to schedule my consultation to occur quickly in just 2 days.  When the consultation occurred in Allen, TX I was greeted by the very gracious Ms. Bradley who also attended.  Mr. Palmer listened to everything I had to say and reviewed the documents I had brought with me.  He gave me honest answers or opinions to all my questions based on his professional experience.  I was provided Mr. Palmer's notes​ taken upon the consultation being concluded, and ​I was provided the ​confidential audio recording of my consultation via email.  I highly recommend Palmer Law Group based on my own experience and suggest other law firms should be providing this type of open service that I had never experienced before.  DM

reviews.text:
The Dana Palmer Law Group recently assisted me with a probate matter in 2025; THEY ARE AWESOME!  The probate issue was resolved by Dana Palmer's Law Group after over 3 years of tears, frustration, turmoil, and a lack of progress working with another law firm who failed to resolve the matter.   I worked with Daniel Bohmer and Terri Shumway who were reliable, consistent, professional, and on top of the situation the entire way! Both Daniel and Terri were always working in my best interest and gave me a great sense of relief knowing that they were working on my behalf to resolve the contested matter that I brought to them in 2023.   The reception staff were always available, efficient, pleasant, and willing to assist me anytime I called.

The law firm was open and willing to work for my cause through the "Contingency Plan" that they were able to offer me. The plan required that I pay 33.3% of the award from the estate once  Dana Palmer Law Group resolved the matter, in which I thought was fair and doable for my situation.    This worked for me because I had already spent thousands of dollars on other lawyers who unfortunately took my money and did not perform their due diligence to resolve my probate matter.   Mr. Palmer's  team worked diligently and consistently every step of the way!  They even called the opposing counseling on a daily basis for months to move the matter forward.  Mr. Bohmer and Ms. Shumway made me feel like they cared about my plight and that my case was important to them.  When needed, they made time for me and acted quickly throughout the entire case.
I would definitely recommend The Dana Palmer Law Group to anyone needing smart, effective, and competent attorney's working for their best interest for any legal need!  I am happy and blessed to have found them after talking with literally over 50 attorney's; I believe that I was guided and blessed to work with them! GIVE THEM A CALL!

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for this review, Mr. McGowan. We are sorry you were dissatisfied. I’d prefer to be able to respond specifically about your reviews, but we will not breach your attorney-client privilege to dispute your assertions. Instead we have to just observe from your review that you seem to have had a difficult time and I suggest to the readers who read your review to consider reading it light of you describing how well you cooperated with us, then consider their option from that point of view. For further context, our process is to thoroughly review any client complaint through several managers who review the situation and work with a dissatisfied client to make things right within all reasonable expectations. Unfortunately, we can’t please everybody. Also, the vast majority of our cases do not require mediation, but some do, and we usually do very well on those mediations. But mediation is required in most counties in North Texas. If a case cannot be settled by negotiations, then mediation is required by the courts.

reviews.text:
Going through a contested divorce as a stay-at-home parent—with significant financial disparity and custody on the line—was one of the most stressful and uncertain times of my life. I honestly don’t know how I would’ve made it through without the support, expertise, and compassion of Thomas Wendt and Jean Skinner.

Thomas was exactly the kind of lawyer I needed: calm, knowledgeable, and steady under pressure. He helped me navigate difficult custody and property division issues with clarity and confidence. Even when things felt overwhelming, Thomas provided reassurance and realistic advice that helped me stay grounded and hopeful.

Jean Skinner, the paralegal on my case, went above and beyond time and time again. She didn’t just handle documents—she was a source of emotional support. She intuitively knew when I needed something to focus on and would assign me tasks to keep my mind active during particularly stressful periods. Her compassion and presence made a real difference during some of my hardest days.

What also stood out to me was how understanding the entire team was of my financial situation. They consistently worked with me to find creative solutions so I could continue receiving their support without it becoming a financial impossibility.

If you're going through a divorce—especially one involving children—I can't recommend Thomas and Jean highly enough. They not only helped me protect my rights as a parent, but they did it with heart and integrity. I'm incredibly grateful for everything they did for me and my family.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dillon Reed,
Thank you so much for your kind words and 5-star review. We’re truly grateful you trusted Thomas and Jean during such a challenging time. It means a lot to know our support, guidance, and compassion made a difference for you and your family. Wishing you continued strength and peace in this next chapter.

Charlie is not only very knowledgeable regarding Texas trusts, wills, and estates law but is also very good at explaining the same throughout the entire engagement process, from the initial phone call all the way through drafting and eventual execution of documents.  He is very diligent and does what he says he will do, either via email communications or during our in-person session.  The signing process went very smoothly and Charlie took the time to explain each provision that was being signed as well as providing further written information and explanation after the fact.  He finally provided a very fulsome binder of the entire package that we ordered and even provided helpful hints as to next steps, such as which family friends to contact and what information may need to be updated with various parties that have an interest, such as insurance and mortgage companies.

Finally, his fees charged for services rendered were very reasonable, given prevailing attorney billing rates and the amount of work Charlie put into our estate planning.

Going through a divorce is one of the hardest experiences a man can face, especially in a family court system that often feels biased before you even walk through the door. During that season of my life, Mark Montgomery wasn’t just my attorney — he was a steady, committed advocate who stood with me when everything felt stacked against me.

Mark worked long hours, dug into every detail, and fought for me with a level of dedication that went far beyond the job description. He listened, he cared, and he made sure I understood every step of the process. In a time when it would have been easy to feel alone, Mark showed up not only as a skilled lawyer but as a genuine friend.

What stood out most was his commitment to fairness. He believes in men’s advocacy, and he isn’t afraid to push back when the system leans the other way. He gave me a voice when I felt like I didn’t have one, and he fought for an outcome that reflected truth, not assumptions.

If you’re a man facing divorce or custody issues and you want someone who will fight for you, stand with you, and treat you with respect, Mark Montgomery is the attorney you want in your corner. I’m grateful for his work and the integrity he brought to one of the most difficult chapters of my life.

I have attempted to contact Mark multiple times since I have legally been an adult and over what I'd say is at least three years I have never received a call back, a returning email or anything that shows a shred of care for his client.
reviews.ownerResponse: I am not aware of who this person is. I have a great reputation for returning calls. So to say I don’t care for someone who I cannot find a record that supports their claim seems unfair. I will say if this person will call my office at 469/952/6500 I will return their call . reviews.text: Express legal funding is a joke when you ask him for what you want they'll give you tell you that they'll give you something closer I asked for 40,000 they said they could do 13,000 then when I submitted my application everything they only gave me $5,000 and then told me that I could apply for a higher amount later and continue to drop the amount they gave me every time so that way they can give you more rates to pay back instead of giving you the one time loan that you ask for at first that's their way of getting as much money from you as they can
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi, Julio, thank you for sharing your experience.

We’d like to clarify a few points for accuracy and explain how approvals work with pre-settlement funding. We never promise or guarantee specific funding amounts. Any numbers discussed before a full underwriting review are estimates. The final approved amount depends on the case details, available insurance coverage, existing funding, and other relevant factors.

Legal funding is also not a mortgage or a traditional loan. Larger advances (such as $40,000) are only approved when the case and coverage support that level of risk. In many situations, we start with a smaller initial advance to help protect a client’s potential settlement and avoid over-funding. Also, taking funding in smaller increments over time can reduce overall cost compared to taking a large amount up front, because you’re only charged on the funds you actually receive.

Finally, additional requests do not increase the rate on prior advances and are not designed to “stack” fees. Each request is reviewed separately, and clients are never obligated to accept any offer.

We understand this can feel frustrating, and we’re happy to explain any approval decision in more detail directly. Our goal is always to provide clear, transparent options so clients can choose what works best for them. We appreciate the opportunity to assist and wish you the best with your case.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Justin, thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re truly sorry for the frustration you experienced during the process.

After reviewing your file, we did follow up with your law firm on December 3. Unfortunately, your account manager was out of the office for personal reasons on December 4 and 5, and their email included an auto-response directing all case-related messages to an alternate contact. It appears your law firm may not have reached out to that contact, which contributed to the holdup.

Your feedback is very important to us, and we’ll use it to improve our internal processes to help prevent situations like this in the future (use email forwarding instead). We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this caused and appreciate your patience. If you’re still interested, we will continue working on your request this Monday morning and do our best to move things forward quickly.

After getting all the information needed form my law firm, I got the money within 24 hours. Despite their interest is too high, they are reliable and recommendable to anybody facing my situation. Better diying tomorrow than today. Again, thank you to all Express Legal Findind staff for your salvation. I paid my debts and supported my family.

reviews.text:
Attorney Lefebvre and her team (Gabriela & Isabel) handled my husband's consular process case (relative petition for residency) with the utmost professionalism, care, and expertise. From the beginning, they were consistently responsive, and knowledgeable—always available to answer our many questions and concerns.

Although most of our communication was through phone and email, we always felt supported. Attorney Lefebvre kept us informed at critical points, and while most day-to-day communication was with her excellent paralegals, she was always copied on emails and stepped in when needed. That level of involvement gave my husband and me real peace of mind.

Due to USCIS delayed timelines, it took short of four years from start to finish (2021-2025). My husband’s case included a prior border entry/attempt that made us very nervous. We consulted with 3 other attorneys before choosing Attorney Lefebvre, and we are so glad we did. Her honesty, and expertise immediately made us feel at ease. She reassured us that as long as we were truthful, we had a strong case—and she was right.

Ahead of the consular interview in Ciudad Juarez, she personally met with my husband to prepare him and went above and beyond to ensure we were ready. Even after that meeting, she remained available by email to answer every follow-up question—always patient and understanding, even when we felt like we were being annoying.

During our time in Ciudad Juarez, Attorney Lefebvre stayed in touch via email and phone, even during the evenings and weekends. Her presence was constant and comforting. When my husband was unexpectedly placed in administrative processing (not due to any missing documents—just officer discretion), she checked in, followed up, and assured us everything would be okay.

Several days later, my husband was approved. We left for the interview at the end of May and made it back home in mid-June, and I can confidently say we could not have done this without her. Attorney Lefebvre and her team were thorough, organized, and truly cared about our outcome. They prepared us flawlessly and guided us EVERY step of the way.

We’re now just waiting for my husband’s green card to arrive in the mail and already plan to hire Attorney Lefebvre again in 3 years for his citizenship process.

My husband and I are forever grateful for the time, dedication, and heart put into our case. If you're looking for an immigration attorney that is honest, compassionate, and exceptionally professional—look no further. This firm is the real deal. They are turly the BEST.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your thorough and kind review! We wish you all the best moving forward and look forward to seeing you in the near future.

Sincerely,

Attorney Liset Lefebvre

I had a consultation with Mr. Freeman of Freeman Law in Frisco on Thursday, May 8th, during which we discussed the complex details of my legal matter. He seemed engaged at the time and requested supporting documents to review the case further. I followed up promptly with everything he asked for.

What followed was radio silence.

Despite three phone calls (two of which were answered by a receptionist who assured me Mr. Freeman would follow up), and three emails (two to his direct email and one to the firm’s general inbox), I have not received a single response, not even a courtesy reply.

To be clear: I was never officially a client, but a prospective client seeking representation. I approached this firm in good faith, with urgency and trust, hoping for professionalism and guidance. What I received instead was complete disregard, no follow-up, and a total lack of communication. Out of 32 reviews leading to an over all 4.9, I expected much more.

Even a simple “we’ve decided not to take the case” would have been better than this level of indifference.

If this is how Freeman Law treats people before they’re even clients, I shudder to think how they might handle time-sensitive matters after a retainer is signed.

Extremely unprofessional and, frankly, disrespectful.

i caught new charges and revoked my probation and was facing prison time and mostly felony charges, but Doug and his team was able to help me from a to z  regarding all of my cases, Doug is very knowledgeable and did an outstanding job! i would recommend them to everybody. because of them i was able to turn my life around and get a second chance at freedom! god bless wilder law firm!

I was in recovery for quite a while and relapsed last July. They say it takes what it takes to get it.  I found myself in handcuffs that night and full of shame the next day.  As most people do, I googled law firms defending people with my charge and the Wilder Law Firm had exceptional reviews (and I read every single review) and I just had a really good feeling. Best decision ever! From the very beginning, Megan and Ronnie didn't make me feel like a criminal and made me feel extremely comfortable from the first interaction. Megan was extremely down to earth and not intimidating, which I thought all lawyers were. She instructed me to take 2 classes and journal all of my AA meetings. Megan had my sponsor also write a letter discussing my progress since my relapse. I had to show up to court 1 time out of the 5+ times I was scheduled, because Megan showed up for me and took everything to the prosecution and my charge was dropped. This was very unexpected and I will be forever grateful for what they did for me!  I was finally able to put this behind me.  Like I said before, it takes what it takes to stay sober, and I think that night had to happen for me to work as hard as I have this past year. I am happier today than I have ever been, my boyfriend (now fiancé) and I are better than ever, and I was able to start a job at a huge hospital in Dallas because of Megan and the firm. Thank you, Megan and Ronnie, for everything you did for me.  I will be forever grateful!

was facing a DWI 2nd as well as an Accident Involving Damage. After speaking with Doug, I felt extremely comfortable with trusting the Wilder Law Firm in handling my cases and guiding me. Not only did he help me understand the legal process, but also want I needed to do in order to put myself in the best position possible moving forward. I was extremely stressed and the very favorable outcome with my case has eased that stress BIG time. Thanks to Doug, Morgan and Ronnie for having an answer anytime I called or text the office. I appreciate you guys, and if I ever hear of anyone needing help or representation I will be sending them to the Wilder law Firm.

Due to the reduced charge, I was able to move from TX to the Midwest to be closer to family and my aging parent. This has impacted my life because I work as an Executive. Having a felony would have prevented me from continuing in my professional career of 25+ years. Doug, Morgan and Ronnie were all compassionate and competent in handling of my case.

Doug is the kind of attorney any falsely accused defendant could dream of having.  His straight no nonsense no bs kind of approach is one everyone dreams of having in situations like this.  Do not waste your time interviewing other attorneys like i did.  Call THE WILDER LAW FIRM and get your case handled!

Doug and the rest of the staff were amazing. They fought for me and had my best interest the entire time. I would suggest anyone who may be experience any type of legal problem to use there services. They will keep fighting for you till the end and no matter how long it take.

I interviewed several other attorneys in the area and feel confident I wouldn't have had such a great outcome if I had hired them. If you or anyone you know needs a trusted Plano DWI lawyer, we’re here to help. We wish you continued success on your new path and are always just a call away if you need us. Thank you again for trusting the Wilder Law Firm!

reviews.text:
We were faced with an unthinkable set of circumstances that turned our life upside down. Our son who has special needs was unfairly arrested facing multiple charges. The police arrested our son against our will in our own home for domestic violence. They didn't care that he suffered from mental illness including severe Tourette's syndrome.

I explained to the police about his mental illness and all they cared about was making an arrest. We explained to the police that our son had certain illnesses beyond his control and we did not want him unfairly arrested. It was about 2.00am and he was arrested, taken out of our own home and off to jail he went.

I called family and friends for advice and help. We called several attorneys in the middle of the night but in our gut we were just not comfortable. We Googled and came across the Wilder Law Firm. After reading countless Google reviews we decided to call them. We met with Doug, Morgan, and Ronnie. They listened to us for what felt like hours. I watched them intently and they genuinely cared and listened. They didn't tell us what we wanted to hear; they told us the truth. They felt the police were wrong and they would fight until this was resolved.

We knew right away that we were going to use them. Well after six months we received a call from Morgan that ALL charges were dropped. Doug, you and your team are amazing!! We as a family we have to give a special personal thank you to Morgan. Without her incredible guidance, hours spent, and knowledge of the law there is no way other than this review could we thank you enough!!!

Simply put; the best Criminal Defense Lawyers in Dallas!

Thank you,
Marina & Blake
reviews.ownerResponse: Your journey is inspiring, and we're honored that you put your trust in us. Supporting families with unique journeys like yours is exactly why we do what we do, and knowing we could make a positive impact fills us with joy.

reviews.text: I was facing a felony DWI 3rd, and found the Wilder Law Firm. Mr. Wilder and Morgan were always available and constantly kept me informed of each step through the process of getting this handled. My felony was dropped down and charged as a misdemeanor. Through this, Mr. Wilder encouraged me to address my Alcoholism through treatment and hard work, which I did. And it has changed my life. I am now sober and NOT a convicted felon. I am proud to say this. I would like to say thank you again to Doug and Morgan for everything they did for me. It is greatly appreciated.

After previously being accused of a serious felony charge, my family reached out to the Wilder Law Firm because of their wonderful reviews and realistic pricing. They were amazing and helped me get my case completely dismissed. They made life through all of this much less stressful and really helped me protect my future from what could have been if a felony like this was on my record. Thank you again!
reviews.ownerResponse: Hearing your journey to a dismissed case with us is just heartwarming! We're so glad we could stand by your side and make a difference in what was an undoubtedly stressful time.  If you ever find yourself in need again or know someone who does, we’re here and ready to help. Wishing you all the best! 🌟

reviews.text:
I’m sure this is an abnormal review but.. I was a juror this past week on a case where Mr. Wilder was the defense attorney. To say he was passionate about his client would truly be an understatement. He laid out the facts of the situation very easily and was able to make the jury see the (rightful)  innocence of his client.  It’s safe to say that it was a great experience watching him in action and I was so moved after delivering our not guilty verdict that I had to come online and look him up. He means business and he got the job DONE.

Long story short: Hire him/his firm if you’re finding yourself dealing with an unjust situation, you won’t regret it.
reviews.ownerResponse: Wow! We are truly thrilled to hear about your positive experience with Mr Wilder. His dedication and skill make him a true asset to our team, and we're so glad you noticed he could make such a difference. Thank you for sharing your kind words!

reviews.text:
Doug is an absolute game changer whose expertise made all the difference. Even the Jury and the Judge appreciated the way he handled the defense.

If you are looking for an attorney, Doug is no brainer!!!!
reviews.ownerResponse: 💖 We're over the moon hearing about your experience with Doug! His expertise and the fact that the Jury and Judge noticed his skillful handling is music to our ears. 🎶 If anyone you know is in search of an incredible attorney, please put them in touch.

reviews.text: I was charged with DWI. The Wilder Law Firm was recommended by another lawyer. The Wilder Law Firm explained the various steps involved in my case and helped me during the process. They helped me obtain a plea bargain of 12 months deferred probation. This outcome has impacted my life by making me more aware of drinking and driving and measures to prevent such behavior. Thank each and every member of The Wilder Law Firm for their time and consideration regarding my personal case.
reviews.authorName: Child of the Most High King

reviews.text: During the initial consultation, Megan not only treated me with respect but also showed genuine concern for my situation. She immediately presented a clear plan and strategy that aimed for the best possible outcome. Her understanding and professional approach made me feel confident that Wilder Law Firm was truly dedicated to achieving the best results for my case. The Wilder Law Firm provided strong and dedicated representation, tirelessly working to secure the best possible outcome for me. They managed all the documentation and legal details, significantly reducing the stress and confusion of dealing with the court system. I particularly appreciated the consistent follow-ups and timely status updates about my case, which kept me informed and gave me peace of mind knowing that everything was being handled professionally. Additionally, they treated my family with kindness and respect throughout the entire process, ensuring we were always well-informed and comfortable at every stage. Thanks to Megan and the dedicated team at Wilder, my case was dismissed! The dismissal of my case has been life-changing, not just for me but for my entire family. It has lifted a tremendous weight off our shoulders, eliminating the constant worry about potential financial burdens and allowing us to focus on our future without the shadow of legal troubles. This outcome has brought us immense relief, closure, and peace of mind, allowing us to move forward with our lives with renewed hope and security. I cannot express enough gratitude to Megan and the entire Wilder team for all they have done to turn a difficult and stressful situation into a thing of the past. Their unwavering professionalism and genuine care for my family’s well-being made a world of difference during this challenging time. I wholeheartedly recommend Wilder Law Firm to anyone who needs compassionate, skilled, and effective legal representation. They truly go above and beyond for their clients.

Update: Motion for early termination of probation has been granted by the court with a Wilder Law Firm's help. I strongly recommend Wilder Law Firm. Thanks again Doug, Megan, Ronnie and Anastacia!

Thanks to Doug, Megan, Anastacia, Ronnie, and Morgan for all of your times and supports for my case. From my first phone conversation with Megan and her willingness to help to my face to face interview with Doug, the Wilder Law Firm has been nothing but helpful. They have provided me with legal advice and handled my case correctly from the beginning which resulted in a satisfactory outcome. I still need Wilder Law Firm's legal help for an early termination of my deferred jurisdiction so will share my experience again once my case was truly over from my perspective.

Mr. Wilder is one of the lawyers who does not do this for the money, but for the passion. I called him in a panic due to an elaborate scam I came tangled up with, and he helped me to uncover it as well as help ease my worries. He did all of this without payment, and was willing to speak for however long I needed. I recommend anyone to go through Wilder Law Firm, especially the man himself. Thank you from the bottom of my heart, Mr. Wilder. You saved my life.

The Wilder Law Firm saved my rear end. I was facing a DWI and was desperate for help. They answered my call and ended up providing awesome representation. I got only 9 months of deferred probation and my interlock removed after only 2 months. The Wilder Law Firm took all the stress off of me with how they handled the case and I have not been worried remotely and felt confident that you would handle it. Now that everything has been squared away and I know what to expect I can move on with my life. These guys were my hero! In the business that I work in total loss for insurance company I deal with a lot of attorneys and my experience has never been good and the people I deal with are frequently shady. The Wilder Law Firm team is so professional and I will give referral to anybody I know to go to them!

I hired the Wilder Law Firm to help me with my DWI case after seeing their great reviews. Throughout the process, I mostly spoke with Morgan and she was super helpful and answered my questions and got back to me in time. I was able to get 20 months of deferred probation which was better than I expected! The overall experience has helped relieve stress in my life.

The reviews were very good for the Wilder Law Firm but when Doug took the time to speak on everything to my wife the day after my DWI arrest, I knew I would be seeking Doug for help. In defending me, his team was able to get me deferred adjudication and interlock device waived. I have been stressing about this for months but Doug's team took care of it and got me the best outcome I could hope for. Thank you Wilder Law Firm for taking personal care of me in this situation. I never felt like just a number or unimportant. Doug and his team really helped me to get the best possible outcome and make this process as easy as possible and I am extremely grateful.

I had a DWI charge with high bac number. I ended up hiring the Wilder Law Firm to help me based upon their reviews with good outcomes. The Wilder Law Firm gave me the knowledge of what I needed to do to have the least amount of negative impact on my life. They got me probably the best outcome I could have received and was able to get the interlock device removed. This has given me peace of mind. The deferred probation has given me the chance to not have a conviction on my record after the probation is over. Thank you all at the Wilder Law Firm for your patience with us. Our attitude at times was coming from our really high stress and fear. Thank you for everything you've done.

I prayed that Angels would surround my legal team during my case. Turns out Megan is a fierce Angel herself!!! She shed light on the EXTRA bits in my case that lead to total dismissal. Eternally grateful!

For the past three years, this law firm has went ABOVE AND BEYOND for my family. We were facing UNFATHOMABLE accusations brought on one of my family members and the Wilder Law Firm fought HARD get our case dismissed. Mainly, Miss Morgan! She was the fiercest in helping us. Spending even her personal time after work to help us. She didn’t quit, she didn’t put us on the back burner when our case seemed like it would never end. She truly and WHOLE HEARTEDLY put her ALL in this for us. She hunted the key piece to get our case dismissed and although things do take time, she stayed on top of it day in and day out!! My family truly values and admires her honesty, (even when it was hard to hear), diligence and dedication to not only our case, but also my family. She truly was our “Charlie’s Angel”! MORGAN, THANK YOU, FROM THE BOTTOM OF MY HEART!! ❤️
The Wilder Law Firm has become like family to us through this whole thing. The patience and understanding and the care with our children through this terrible thing looming over us, listening ears to ramblings and venting at whatever time of day or night. They are all truly special to my family and you’re making a mistake by not letting them handle whatever case you may have! Circumstances aside, WE LOVE THE WILDER LAW FIRM!!!

After recently being arrested for DUI, I found the Wilder Law Firm through their online reviews. Doug, Megan, and Morgan were able to negotiate a satisfactory outcome! This has made me reevaluate how I live my life and make some needed changes. Thank you to everyone for being patient with my stubborn ways! Kind Regards.
Thank you thank you thank you. Huge help with a difficult time in my life. You guys were super helpful with all of my questions and great with time management and communication.

I called to schedule a consultation and felt lucky they were able to get me in the same day. Boom, I was asked to complete their intake form and send over my current divorce decree so that Tiffany can review and prepare prior to our zoom meeting.

Tiffany was not prepared at all. She was not fully engaged in our conversation due to her trying to read my decree during our meeting. I found myself repeating myself multiple times because of this. She had no sense of empathy. I was literally in tears and emotional as I explained my situation to her. After wiping my eyes and refocusing them on the camera due to dead silence I see Tiffany looking down just scrolling away in her phone. You can see the glare in the picture below.

I am not even sure if I received sound legal advice. This was the easiest $250 she made! Good luck if you hire her.
Very great firm. Thorough work. Communication is top-notch. Well experienced in law. Has the clients best interest at hand.
She is a good lawyer but also sweet and treats her clients as family. We will always use her!
She was really helpful to me. Thanks! 👍

I found Ms. Kamuche after having a bad experience with my former attorney back in 2020; when my brother passed away. As soon as she got my case she was on it; filing all the paperwork and having everything ready for me to do on my end. The process has been smooth without issues and now that we are almost done with probate I look back and I'm very appreciative for her staff and above all Ms. Kamuche. She is very communicative alongside her staff and let's you know up front whats going on without any doubt. If later needed I will utlize her services, hopefully not lol but if I do I know who to call. Thanks Ms. Kamuche and staff!
Tiffany and her staff are very professional. I highly recommend using her services.

Although easily accessible and responsive to  inquiries regarding my divorce case (as any retained attorney should be.) Tiffany was unable to get the job done. As a client, I did not feel adequately represented. What she could not do in 6 months and thousands of dollars spent, I was able to do simply by  representing myself.

Where do I start?  We hired Tiffany to represent my son in a divorce/custody suit.  Tiffany for a lack of better words " put the hammer down" each time my son appeared.  Her knowledge in the field of divorce and custody was outstanding.  She stopped at nothing to ensure my son's case was heard very clear.  AND he won...  I would recommend and will forever refer and utilize her services myself.   Thank you Tiffany Kamuche, JOB WELL DONE!

It’s been three years since my husband and I employed Tiffany to help with our family law matter. Tiffany stopped at nothing to make sure we were satisfied with the outcomes. I remember emailing her in the wee hours of the night and getting a prompt reply. Tiffany is truly one of kind. She’s passionate about her craft, knowledgeable, and honest. If you have a family law matter, you definitely want her on your side.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for allowing our office to serve your family and sharing these kind words.

reviews.text: Tiffany Kamuche is one of the most hard-working, thorough and ethical attorneys I know.  She is client-focused and works tirelessly to provide sound advice and legal representation to each of her clients.  Further, she cares about her client's circumstance.  She is compassionate, understanding and passionate about helping her clients.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your continued support!

reviews.text: Tiffany is professional as it gets when it comes to seeking legal advice. One thing that I truly appreciate and admire about her service is that she goes over and beyond to provide you the best solution to your issue. If she is not sure, she will seek every possible resource to give you concrete answers. That lets you know that she doesn't settle for the as is. Thank you for your expertise Tiffany.  I will continue to support and refer your services among my peers.

reviews.text: I am a practicing attorney in the DFW area, and I refer every person seeking family law and/or estate planning legal work to Kamuche Law. Tiffany is an excellent attorney. reviews.text: The firm was very professional and knowledgeable. Had no problem with working long distance and solving issues and taking all the time needed to answer your questions, never felt rushed. Would recommend to anyone seeking legal advice.
